registry.notes: Added 2026-03-15: identified in Next 25 analysis as missing from original 181-word list.
registry.description: Contempt is the inner dismissal of another as beneath consideration — the attitude that treats someone as worthless, ridiculous, or unworthy of respect. The Hebrew vocabulary covers to lighten, to despise, to taunt, to reject as of no account. Contempt is one of the most socially toxic of inner states because it is incompatible with genuine relationship: the contemptuous person cannot love the one they despise. Jesus's warning in the Sermon on the Mount treats contempt (calling someone a fool) with the same gravity as murder, because both proceed from the same inner dismissal of the other's worth.

terms.medium_def:
1) to be slight, be swift, be trifling, be of little account, be light
1a) (Qal)
1a1) to be slight, be abated (of water)
1a2) to be swift
1a3) to be trifling, be of little account
1b) (Niphal)
1b1) to be swift, show oneself swift
1b2) to appear trifling, be too trifling, be insignificant
1b3) to be lightly esteemed
1c) (Piel)
1c1) to make despicable
1c2) to curse
1d) (Pual) to be cursed
1e) (Hiphil)
1e1) to make light, lighten
1e2) to treat with contempt, bring contempt or dishonour
1f) (Pilpel)
1f1) to shake
1f2) to whet
1g) (Hithpalpel) to shake oneself, be moved to and fro

Look , when the messenger comes , shut the door and hold the door fast against him. Is not the sound of his master’s feet behind him ?”
